body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_2)
Regime data: VIX 16.06 | HY OAS 270 | Breadth 81.8%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — source failure or stale data (G1_data_quality: observed ['alpha_vantage_news'] threshold no failures/stale)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — source failure or stale data (G1_data_quality: observed ['alpha_vantage_news'] threshold no failures/stale)
SPY: NO_TRADE — source failure or stale data (G1_data_quality: observed ['alpha_vantage_news'] threshold no failures/stale)
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: issues: alpha_vantage_news

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows a modestly elevated VIX at 16.06 with a steep contango between 9-day (13.39) and 3-month (19.09) implied volatility, signaling short-term caution but longer-term risk premium remains elevated. HY OAS at 270 bps and strong breadth at 81.8% suggest underlying market resilience despite geopolitical jitters from Iran’s new strikes, which are pressuring the Nasdaq and S&P 500 lower at the open. SPY at $753.22 with IV at 25.41% and negative sentiment reflects this risk-off tone, but NVDA ($208.94, IV 50.4%, sentiment +1) and AMZN ($246.66, IV 51.04%, sentiment +1) remain bullish, driven by the AI infrastructure boom and robust revenue growth expectations, respectively. The near-term catalyst to watch is the Treasury balance release today and upcoming CPI/PPI data this week, which could recalibrate inflation expectations and impact rates, currently at 4.16% (2Y) and 4.54% (10Y), posing a risk to growth stocks if inflation surprises on the upside. The key risk building is geopolitical escalation and inflationary pressures from India’s accelerating inflation at 4.38%, which could tighten financial conditions further. System is in warmup with 24 more daily runs before IV rank gates activate, so trade signals remain preliminary.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes

NVDA.sentiment_driver: AI Infrastructure Boom Drives Taiwan Suppliers Toward U.S. Manufacturing Expansi

AMZN.sentiment_driver: MercadoLibre Will Join Amazon in the $50 Billion Revenue Club

India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts

Regular water isn't enough: The corporate gold rush for ‘functional hydration’
Fed Chair Warsh faces the hot seat as Congress presses for a read on the economy
‘Day-to-day dread’ haunts frustrated job seekers in era of low hiring
Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high

World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with JPM, WFC, GS, BAC, and MS reporting between July 14-15. Investors will focus on credit quality and trading revenue for signals on financial sector resilience amid current volatility (VIX 16.06, HY OAS 270 bps). Watch for any surprises that could ripple into broader equity sentiment, particularly impacting SPY given financials’ weight, while subdued market odds suggest muted immediate moves for NVDA and AMZN.
narratives.next_week: Next week features key tech and industrial earnings, notably GOOGL and TSLA on July 22, alongside real estate and materials names like CCI and FCX. GOOGL’s results will be a barometer for ad spending and cloud growth, critical for Communication Services and tech sector momentum that supports NVDA and AMZN. TSLA’s delivery and margin outlook will also inform consumer discretionary risk appetite, with potential spillover into SPY’s cyclicals exposure.
narratives.next_month: Looking further ahead, July 28-31 brings heavyweight tech earnings from MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, and META, alongside the FOMC meeting and July PCE inflation data. The FOMC’s likely hold at 3.50% and inflation prints will set the tone for market direction, influencing growth versus value rotations. Strong tech results could reinforce NVDA and AMZN leadership, while inflation risks and Fed policy will remain key cross-asset drivers for SPY’s broader trajectory.

bull.trigger: JPM beats earnings and revenue estimates
bull.implication: SPY rises on financial sector strength; NVDA and AMZN mildly positive due to market sentiment.

base.implication: SPY stable; NVDA and AMZN unaffected.

bear.trigger: JPM misses earnings or issues cautious outlook
bear.implication: SPY declines on financial sector weakness; NVDA and AMZN dragged by risk-off sentiment.
